prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "俯卧撑" (push-up) exercise, side view, facing right. Person posture: Athletic person at the bottom of a standard push-up: hands on the ground slightly wider than shoulder-width with arms bent so that elbows are at about 45 degrees from the torso (not flared out); chest hovering just above the ground; toes on the ground, feet hip-width apart; body in one straight line from heels to head with no sag and no piking; head in line with spine; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: pectoralis major (chest) and triceps brachii (back of arms). Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: anterior deltoid (front shoulder) and serratus anterior and core muscles. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "胸大肌" pointing to the pectoralis major (chest); "肱三头肌" pointing to the triceps brachii; "三角肌前束 / 核心" pointing to the anterior deltoid and core area. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
